questions.text: The process of breakdown of food in the cell with the release of energy is called:
questions.explanation: Cellular respiration takes place in the cells of all organisms to provide energy.

questions.text: Respiration that uses oxygen is called:
questions.explanation: Aerobic respiration breaks down glucose into carbon dioxide and water using oxygen.

questions.text: During heavy exercise, we get cramps in the legs due to the accumulation of:
questions.explanation: The partial breakdown of glucose in muscles during anaerobic respiration produces lactic acid.
Yeast breaks down glucose into alcohol and carbon dioxide without using oxygen.

questions.text: Cockroaches breathe through small openings on the sides of their body called:
questions.explanation: Spiracles lead to a network of air tubes called tracheae for gas exchange.
The skin of an earthworm feels moist and slimy, allowing gases to pass through easily.

questions.text: The large, muscular sheet that forms the floor of the chest cavity is the:
questions.explanation: The movement of the diaphragm and the rib cage helps in the process of breathing.

questions.text: Exhaled air contains a higher percentage of:
questions.explanation: During respiration, oxygen is consumed and carbon dioxide is produced as a byproduct.

questions.text: Fish breathe with the help of:
questions.explanation: Gills are projections of the skin that help fish use oxygen dissolved in water.
Ribs move up and out to increase the space in the chest cavity, letting air rush into the lungs.
